title: AddressWatchRegistry: deregister() never called on session completion
description:
Location: cli/src/commands.rs — deregister() method exists but is never called. Sessions are registered at startup and on creation, but never removed when reaching terminal states (Confirmed/Failed/Expired). The registry accumulates stale mappings indefinitely.

Why it matters: When nie-ghf (block watcher) scans compact blocks and calls registry.lookup(), it will get hits for completed sessions. Additionally, a long-running wallet grows the registry without bound.

Mitigation: deregister() must be called in dispatch_payment() whenever a session transitions to Confirmed/Failed/Expired, passing session.address. The call site is in nie-ghf and nie-iiy (where the Confirmed transition happens). File a note on nie-ghf and nie-iiy descriptions to require deregister() call.

Note: This is incomplete-by-design (nie-ghf not yet implemented). The issue is that the description of nie-ghf and nie-iiy does not explicitly mention deregister() — that omission is the bug.
close_reason: Documented deregister() requirement in nie-ghf and nie-iiy bead notes
